success_surface: Sheet-level structural manipulation with text-based row deletion
effective_behaviors: Implemented case-insensitive text search using .lower() normalization on both cell values and search terms
Identified target anchor row before performing any structural modifications
Calculated dynamic deletion range relative to anchor point (rows 1 to target_row-1)
Preserved the anchor row and all subsequent rows during deletion operation
Ensured output directory existence before file save operation
Saved modified workbook to new output path without altering source file
Logged critical milestones: search results, deletion count, and save confirmation
Included error handling for cases where search pattern was not found
evidence: Used 'invoice no.' in cell_value.lower() for case-insensitive comparison across all searched cells
Set target_row variable through iteration before calling ws.delete_rows()
Called ws.delete_rows(1, target_row - 1) to delete only rows above the found anchor
Printed f"Found 'Invoice No.' at row {target_row}" before modification to validate discovery
Used os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True) to guarantee output directory exists
Loaded wb from source path but saved to completely different output_path
Printed confirmation messages after each major step (found, deleted, saved)

memory_items.title: Case-Insensitive String Search Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When searching for text content in spreadsheet cells, normalize both the cell value and search term to lowercase for reliable matching
memory_items.generalization: Always apply .lower() or similar normalization to both sides of text comparisons to handle case variations in user queries or file contents

memory_items.title: Anchor-Based Row Deletion Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Locate the target anchor row first, then calculate deletion range as (1 to anchor_row - 1) to preserve the anchor and all following data
memory_items.generalization: For structural edits based on content markers, always find the anchor position dynamically before computing modification boundaries

memory_items.title: Progressive Operation Logging
memory_items.lesson: Log milestone events at key stages: pattern discovery, modification scope, and final persistence to enable debugging and verification
memory_items.generalization: Include print/logging statements after each critical operation to track execution flow and verify expected outcomes

memory_items.title: Non-Destructive File Workflow
memory_items.lesson: Always create output directory before writing and save modified workbooks to a new path while keeping the source untouched
memory_items.generalization: Maintain source file integrity by separating read and write operations into different file paths with explicit directory preparation

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Case-Insensitive Text Search**: Apply .lower() normalization to both cell values and search terms when matching text content to handle case variations reliably.
- **Anchor-Based Modifications**: Identify target anchor rows/positions through content search before calculating dynamic modification boundaries (e.g., delete rows 1 to anchor-1).
- **Progressive Operation Logging**: Log critical milestones (discovery, action count, save confirmation) to enable execution tracking and debugging.
- **Non-Destructive Workflows**: Ensure output directory exists before saving; write modified workbooks to new paths without altering source files.
